message:
fix(workflows/handlers): bring CompatRuntime trait into scope in test helper

The test helper that builds a current-thread runtime for step-
execution scaffolding calls `RuntimeBuilder::current_thread()` which
returns a CompatRuntime trait object — and then chains `.enable_all()
.build()` which requires the CompatRuntime methods to be resolvable.

Without `use crate::runtime_compat::CompatRuntime;` in the helper's
local scope, the method-call chain fails to compile when the helper
is the first user of the trait in a given test file. Other test
helpers in the same module already had the `use` statement; this one
was missing it (silent gap — it happened to compile because of
ambient imports at the time, but a follow-up refactor elsewhere
broke the transitive resolution).

Bring the trait into the helper's local scope explicitly. The `use`
is inside the fn body (not at module level) to keep the trait
visibility scoped to where it's actually needed.

One-line change, zero behavioral impact — if it compiled before, it
still compiles and behaves identically. If it DIDN'T compile before
(which is the symptom that motivated this patch), now it does.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
